description: §52.15.5 (S255) Leak 1 regression — the SSR-seed omission uses the STRUCTURED row-scope predicate (`queryInterpolationsAreServerAmbientOnly`), not a naive substring. An auth-scoped `?{ SELECT * FROM posts WHERE body LIKE '%@currentUser%' }` carries the literal text `@currentUser` OUTSIDE any `${...}` interpolation, so it is param-free + UNSCOPED (not row-scoped). The naive `q.includes('@currentUser')` would have mis-classified it row-scoped and KEPT it in the anon SSR seed (a cross-user leak while the lint reported it safe). Here the E-ADAPTER SSR path invokes the REAL compose handler anonymously: the posts rows are ABSENT from the first paint + `window.__scrml_ssr_state` seed; a sibling PUBLIC `@widgets` (auth=none) is NOT over-omitted (its rows ARE server-rendered + seeded). The I-SSR-AUTH-SCOPED-CLIENT-HYDRATED info lint fires on @posts.
rationale: §52.15.5: the omission predicate MUST provably coincide with the I-SSR lint — both use `queryInterpolationsAreServerAmbientOnly`, which counts `@currentUser` ONLY inside a live `${...}` interpolation. A literal `@currentUser` in SQL string data is not row-scope, so the query is unscoped → omitted from the anon seed → client-hydrated behind the gated /__serverLoad fetch.
